title: Aporum marivelense
intro: Aporum marivelense, also known as the Mariveles orchid, is a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various ailments such as skin infections and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Aporum marivelense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. Traditionally, Aporum marivelense is used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and sore throat.
taxonomy: Aporum marivelense is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. This epiphytic orchid is native to the Philippines. It belongs to the genus Aporum, which consists of various species of orchids. The classification of Aporum marivelense is further supported by its botanical name, which follows the International Code of Nomenclature for algae, fungi, and plants. Aporum marivelense is considered an endemic species to the Philippines, meaning it is found only in this region.
uses: Aporum marivelense has been reported to have various medicinal properties. However, there is limited information available on its specific uses.
